reasoning: The NFCI has experienced a strong 10-week downward trend (looser conditions), moving from -0.383 in April to -0.488 by June 20. Short-term momentum strongly suggests financial conditions will remain low or decrease slightly over the next few weeks, keeping probabilities for June 29 and July 22 below 0.5. By September, historical cyclicality and mean-reversion strongly come into play. The index operates in a rough [-0.51, -0.36] range, and at -0.488 it is near the likely trough. The June 18 FOMC meeting maintained rates at 4.25-4.50% and introduced a hawkish note regarding "stagflationary pressures," which increases the probability of markets adjusting to fewer rate cuts, tightening financial conditions. Thus, P(increase) for mid and late year dates crosses above 0.5. For the most distant date (December), probabilities regress back toward 0.5, adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon.
